Cojoba glabra, also known as Cojoba, is a plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. 

Cojoba glabra has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to help with weight loss, reduce inflammation, and lower cholesterol levels due to its health benefits.

The bioactive constituents of Cojoba glabra include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

Cojoba glabra is often used to make herbal preparations, including tea, capsules, and infusions, which are available in various health food stores and online retailers.
